name: Doodle Board — whiteboard, paint, sketch, draw, and get creative in VR & MR

reviews.review_description:
I genuinely wanted to enjoy Doodle Board, but after spending time with it, I came away extremely disappointed. This is one of the most frustrating VR experiences I've played on Meta Quest.
The first thing that stood out to me was how low-effort the game feels. The core concept has potential, but the execution feels rushed and unfinished. Many features that should be standard in any drawing application are either missing or locked behind payments. Having to pay just to access basic functions like fill tools, undo, and redo is ridiculous. These are fundamental features that most drawing games and applications include by default, yet here they are treated as premium content.
Another major issue is the amount of assets that appear to be copied or taken from other sources. The game lacks a unique identity and often feels like a collection of reused content rather than something that was carefully designed from the ground up. It gives the impression that more effort went into monetization than into creating a quality experience.
The moderation and lobby system are also terrible. Players can vote-kick others for little to no reason, and this system is extremely easy to abuse. What's worse is that being kicked can result in a one-day ban from public lobbies, even if you did absolutely nothing wrong. I experienced situations where players seemed to kick people simply because they felt like it, and innocent players end up being punished. A moderation system that allows abuse while penalizing victims is unacceptable.
The game also lacks polish in general. Controls can feel awkward, features are limited unless you spend more money, and there isn't enough content to keep players engaged for very long. Instead of feeling like a creative sandbox, it feels like a collection of restrictions designed to push players toward purchases.
Overall, Doodle Board feels unfinished, heavily monetized, and poorly moderated. Locking basic drawing tools behind payments, allowing abusive vote-kicks that can lead to bans, and relying on what appears to be a large amount of reused assets makes this difficult to recommend to anyone. There are far better creative VR experiences available on Meta Quest that respect players' time and don't charge extra for basic functionality.
I cannot recommend this game in its current state

Your game itself, with the drawing, isn’t all that bad. Though I wish the pro features could be done just a little less. Also, I like how we can use 2 buttons on the controller in case one is broken.

Anywho, the reason why the game SUCKS in my opinion is the way public’s are managed. only 5 public lobbies. Seriously? For this community that’s it? Also, anyone can be owner of a lobby. If the owner ends up being a rude player, that’s 1 whole public off the list. Not to mention there is nothing to be done about grief. What if someone doesn’t save it? Then what? We loose everything? The undo button can only go back so many times.

Oh yeah, and the price of this pro feature is unfair. The game is too low quality for that crap.
If you guys just sat up and maybe looked at your game and its community and did something about the problem instead of adding more pro features, maybe this would be an amazing game.

I really think this is a good game to chill with friends, draw anything your imagination can create and its so many colors and the stuff you pay for is so cheap really fun game i would recommend to people who wants to talk with people and draw. FIVE STARRRRSSSS

this game i so much fun and you can get pro for only 1 dollar a month unlike obnoxious games having you pay 30 for a single thing the owner is really nice if i could give this 1000 stars i would

every time i try to draw and i move my controller fast it starts cutting out i don't know if this is a bug or if it's intentional because the pro thing pops up as soon as it does this and it's really starting to make me mad how many drawing apps for vr put paywals behind their games.

developer_response.body: Thanks for reporting. We don't paywall drawing in the app. It sounds like a bug and I'd be happy to help figure out the issue. Send me a message at strictly_jgold.

although Multi player is somewhat buggy, it's still super fun to every once in a while to hop on call or in game with a bunch of other doodlers and compete with eachother in monthly contests! even though I didn't use a tutorial, it was super easy to understand how everything worked!

reviews.review_description: Hey, I'm Strictly, the other owner with Josh. We're always improving the app and working hard to make it a safe and fun space for players to doodle together. If you have any feedback, doodle it and hit the "Send to Josh" button.

Hi I tried it it's good, I know you guys are working hard! Keep going I support you it just needs some smoothness in drawing and it's better to draw with a pen I think it will be better than a controller because my hand shakes and when I'm close to the board I can't control it well and also I hope you will soon provide brushes for drawing and different sizes and shapes I know the application is in its beginning but you did a really great job don't stop 👏🏽❤️
This game is great, if you love drawing, it’s even better you can play this game with friends as well!

If you’re looking for a simple VR whiteboard to brainstorm and collaborate with others to visually build on your ideas, then look no further! Doodle Board is interactive, easy to use, and plenty of fun! When you’re looking for something more engaging than a video call, this is the way to go…

Hopefully we’ll get to see this app integrated into other apps one day!
This game is absolutely amazing i love it so much its calming and great for multiple reasons!
I get to express myself. I love creating drawings with my dad.
Sometimes the best apps / tools are the most focused. Fast, snappy, responsive, serene, productive. If you want an ideal collaboration environment this is it!
I teach my buddy LLMs. Great experience.

Getting ideas out of your head is super important. Getting them into someone else's head is even better. Combining brains on a simple open canvas is the best—and this app does all three.

No frills, high performance, super easy to use.  The focus is on simple expression and communication, rather than mastery or widgets with a high learning curve. It's very accessible and comfortable, and serves as a great foundation for co-discovery and ideation, brainstorming, storytelling, and visual communications in general.

Best part, the markers never run dry, and clean slates are instantaneous and leave no residue.
This is a Great app to get your ideas out and be creative.  Anything rom having Fun to Having a meeting… you can do it all with this app shoutout to the devs for there continuing support

What? Wait a minute! Why haven’t I heard of this till now?
I visited the doodle board, and think it’s Awesome! 
Love it!

reviews.review_title: A Bold Step for Virtual Collaboration
reviews.review_description: As a builder of virtual collab tools myself, I’m impressed by Doodle Board’s potential. Its intuitive interface and smooth performance make brainstorming and teamwork in VR effortless. This app feels like the start of something transformative—I’m excited to see how it evolves!

Makes sketching concepts super easy with its virtual whiteboard/ canvas!  As a visual thinker, it's incredibly helpful for me to have a space to work through ideas. I love how simple it is to save sketches for later or export them to email. 

Collaborating in a shared room is easy and fun - perfect for working through ideas or drawing together.

The peaceful, immersive environment helps me stay focused (yay day + night mode!), but I also love the option of pass-through mode that lets me stay connected to my surroundings (and lets me pet my dog while I draw!). 

The devs are also so kind and responsive. Whether you have a fun idea or need help with an issue, they're quick to reply. Excited to see how Doodle Board continues to grow! Def recommending to friends 🎉🎨✍️

As the world has tilted towards remote/hybrid working, certain aspects of creative teamwork have been strained or lost. Doodle Board is an intriguing step towards bringing back some elements of free-form collaboration for those who can’t congregate in person, due to geography or other reasons. Dialogue where you can see where people are looking, easy collaborative sketching, even just visibly gesturing towards something on the board restore a studio feel in small but relevant ways. Interestingly, being in VR also helps to reduce distractions (who hasn’t checked their email while on a zoom meeting?)  Looking forward to seeing this app’s continued development going forward.
I think  they did a amazing job at this app and honestly I'm excited for them to launch it btw Josh is so amazing and sweet

Quick and Intuitive. Easy to get your saved board from VR to any other "R"eality with the send to email feature.

Also, The Dev's are listening, so give Josh a chance to hear your feedback and see where it goes... Doodle On!
